incidents.summary: Payment service p99 latency jumped from 200ms to 8s at 14:30 UTC. Traced to payment-provider API returning 503 for all requests. Our network was healthy, other dependencies responded normally. Payment-provider status page confirmed ongoing incident. Activated cached payment fallback until they recovered at 16:15 UTC.

Order database connection pool exhausted on orders-db. Active connections at 200/200, zero idle. New queries queueing for 30s+ before timing out. The database itself was responsive for existing connections — bottleneck was in the application-side pool. Traced to a connection leak in the error handling path of the bulk import endpoint.

Auth service TLS certificate expired at midnight. All HTTPS connections returning handshake errors. Service was healthy on HTTP but unreachable over HTTPS. cert-manager renewal had silently failed 3 days prior. Renewed certificate manually and redeployed.

Search service OOM killed 4 times in 6 hours. Memory grew from 512MB to 4GB monotonically regardless of traffic level. Heap dump showed unbounded growth in SearchResultCache entries — cache eviction was disabled by a config change last week. Traffic was stable throughout.

Cart service error rate jumped to 45% after ConfigMap update at 11:20 UTC. No code deployment occurred — container image unchanged. The config change set max_connections to 0, effectively blocking all database access. Reverting the ConfigMap restored service immediately. Code rollback had no effect.

Inventory service latency degraded gradually over 2 hours. Downstream inventory-db query times rose from 5ms to 2s. The calling service request rate matched inbound — no retry amplification. Root cause was a missing index after the Tuesday migration dropped it inadvertently.

API gateway returning 502 errors to 60% of requests. Load balancer health check path had been changed to /ready which didn't exist on the current version. All backend pods were healthy and responding to direct requests. Fixing the health check path in the ingress config resolved the issue.

Notification service hammering email-provider with retries at 12x normal rate. The provider was slightly slow (p99 800ms vs normal 200ms) but the aggressive retry config turned a minor slowdown into a full outage. Disabling retries immediately relieved the provider and restored service.

Shipping service deploy v2.14.3 introduced a NullPointerException in the tracking endpoint. Error rate went from 0.1% to 38% exactly at deploy time. Only shipping-service affected — all dependencies healthy. Rolled back to v2.14.2, errors ceased immediately.

Recommendation service experiencing intermittent duplicate writes to suggestions table. Errors only appeared under concurrent load — single-threaded tests passed cleanly. At 50 concurrent workers: 5% duplicate key violations. At 10 workers: zero errors. Missing SELECT FOR UPDATE on the upsert path.

Services in us-east-1a lost connectivity to catalog-service in us-east-1c. TCP SYN timeouts — not application errors. Services within us-east-1a communicated normally. Traceroute showed blackhole at hop 4. Failover to us-east-1b replica resolved customer impact while networking team investigated.

Flash sale drove traffic to 8x normal on product-service. Memory usage tracked request rate perfectly — 4GB at peak vs 500MB baseline. OOM kills during peak, HPA scaled from 3 to 12 replicas, errors resolved. Memory returned to 500MB after traffic normalized. No leak pattern.
